heading: Definitions
label: 12473 Definitions
notes: References in TextThe Cranston-Gonzalez National Affordable Housing Act, referred to in par. (1)(E), (F), is , , . Title II of the Act, known as the HOME Investment Partnerships Act, is classified principally to subchapter II (§ 12721 et seq.) of chapter 130 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. Subtitle D of title VIII of the Act, known as the AIDS Housing Opportunity Act, is classified generally to chapter 131 (§ 12901 et seq.) of Title 42. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. children.label: A
children.text: under sections 1715e, 1715k, 1715(d)(3), 1715(d)(4), 1715n(e), 1715v, or 1715z–1 of title 12;1So in original. Probably should be “section”.1ll

children.label: B
children.text: under ;section 1701s of title 12

children.label: C
children.text: under ;section 1701q of title 12

children.label: D
children.text: under section 811 of the Cranston-Gonzales National Affordable Housing Act ();242 U.S.C. 8013So in original. Probably should be “Cranston-Gonzalez”.2

children.label: E
children.text: under title II of the Cranston-Gonzales National Affordable Housing Act [ et seq.];242 U.S.C. 12721

children.label: F
children.text: under subtitle D of title VIII of the Cranston-Gonzalez National Affordable Housing Act ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 12901

children.label: G
children.text: under title I of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1974 ( et seq.); or42 U.S.C. 5301

children.label: H
children.text: under ;section 1437f of title 42

the term “continuum of care” means a community plan developed to organize and deliver housing and services to meet the specific needs of people who are homeless as they move to stable housing and achieve maximum self-sufficiency;
the term “low-income housing assistance voucher” means housing assistance described in ;section 1437f of title 42
the term “public housing” means housing described in ;section 1437a(b)(1) of title 42
the term “public housing agency” means an agency described in ;section 1437a(b)(6) of title 42

children.label: A
children.text: mean an individual who lacks a fixed, regular, and adequate nighttime residence; and

children.label: I
children.text: is sharing the housing of other persons due to loss of housing, economic hardship, or a similar reason;

children.label: II
children.text: is living in a motel, hotel, trailer park, or campground due to the lack of alternative adequate accommodations;

children.label: III
children.text: is living in an emergency or transitional shelter;

children.label: IV
children.text: is abandoned in a hospital; or

children.label: V
children.text: is awaiting foster care placement;

children.label: ii
children.text: an individual who has a primary nighttime residence that is a public or private place not designed for or ordinarily used as a regular sleeping accommodation for human beings; or

children.label: iii
children.text: migratory children (as defined in ) who qualify as homeless under this section because the children are living in circumstances described in this paragraph;section 6399 of title 20

the term “homeless service provider” means a nonprofit, nongovernmental homeless service provider, such as a homeless shelter, a homeless service or advocacy program, a tribal organization serving homeless individuals, or coalition or other nonprofit, nongovernmental organization carrying out a community-based homeless or housing program that has a documented history of effective work concerning homelessness;
the term “tribally designated housing” means housing assistance described in the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act of 1996 ( et seq.); and25 U.S.C. 4101

the term “tribally designated housing entity” means a housing entity described in the Native American Housing Assistance and Self-Determination Act of 1996 ();25 U.S.C. 4103(21)3So in original. The semicolon probably should be a period.3
